sections.headline: GCC markets mixed as oil prices dip 2.3%
sections.summary: GCC stock markets showed mixed performance on Wednesday, with Saudi Arabia's Tadawul falling 0.8% and Dubai's DFM gaining 0.5%, as Brent crude slipped to $82.10 per barrel amid demand concerns.
sections.insight: The divergence reflects ongoing investor caution tied to oil price volatility, though UAE markets remain supported by strong real estate and tourism inflows.

sections.headline: Saudi non-oil PMI hits 58.2 in April
sections.summary: Saudi Arabia's non-oil Purchasing Managers' Index rose to 58.2 in April from 57.8 in March, marking the 38th consecutive month of expansion, led by new orders and output growth.
sections.insight: Sustained PMI readings above 55 underscore robust private sector momentum, reinforcing the resilience of Vision 2030-driven diversification away from hydrocarbons.

sections.headline: UAE GDP growth revised up to 5.1% for 2026
sections.summary: The UAE central bank revised its 2026 GDP growth forecast upward to 5.1% from 4.8%, citing strong performance in tourism, logistics, and financial services, with non-oil GDP expected to expand 5.6%.
sections.insight: The upward revision signals deepening economic diversification and positions the UAE as a regional growth leader, attracting foreign capital and talent.

sections.headline: Fintech funding in GCC surges 34% to $1.2B in Q1
sections.summary: Fintech investment in the GCC reached $1.2 billion in Q1 2026, up 34% year-on-year, led by Saudi Arabia's $450 million and the UAE's $380 million, with digital payments and BNPL dominating deal flow.
sections.insight: The surge reflects regulatory tailwinds and rising digital adoption, positioning the GCC as a top global fintech hub, though scaling remains a challenge for late-stage startups.

sections.headline: Saudi Aramco maintains 12.4 mbpd capacity target
sections.summary: Saudi Aramco confirmed it will keep its maximum sustainable capacity at 12.4 million barrels per day, despite OPEC+ production cuts, as it focuses on gas expansion and downstream investments.
sections.insight: The capacity hold signals strategic patience, balancing market share preservation with long-term demand uncertainty and the Kingdom's shift toward gas and petrochemicals.

sections.headline: Dubai real estate transactions hit $12.5B in April
sections.summary: Dubai recorded $12.5 billion in real estate transactions in April 2026, up 18% year-on-year, driven by off-plan sales in Expo City and luxury villa demand in Palm Jumeirah.
sections.insight: Sustained double-digit growth underscores Dubai's appeal as a safe-haven for global capital, though rising supply could moderate price gains in mid-tier segments.
